zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：震荡偏强结构延续
zh.content:
短线呈震荡偏强格局，价格围绕6.29窄幅运行，整体略强于20日均线支撑，买盘动能温和释放多空博弈谨慎短期方向待确认但偏多占优结构稳定

MACD处于零轴上方微弱扩张，动能柱维持正值，显示多头动能占优，但RSI缺失使超买判断不完整，短线依赖均线结构确认趋势延续偏多信号延续短期观察

上方阻力关注6.33前高区域，下方支撑位于6.21及6.00整数关口，若放量突破阻力或打开上行空间需关注成交量配合确认突破有效性

操作建议以回踩6.21分批低吸为主，止损设于6.00下方，若突破6.33可顺势跟进短线多单，控制仓位风险严格执行纪律避免追高操作

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is consolidating in a tight range around 6.29, showing a neutral-to-slightly bullish bias. Price remains just above the 20-day SMA at 6.21, suggesting short-term recovery from prior weakness. However, the extremely narrow 24-hour range indicates low volatility and indecision among traders. The broader structure still reflects a sideways market rather than a strong directional trend, with participants awaiting a catalyst for breakout movement.

MACD prints a small positive reading at 0.0408, indicating mild bullish momentum, though the lack of a visible signal line limits confirmation of trend strength. In absence of RSI data, momentum assessment relies on price behavior and moving average positioning. The slight separation above the SMA20 suggests buyers have a marginal edge, but momentum remains weak and vulnerable to reversal without a rise in volume or volatility.

Immediate support is found near the session low at 6.2866, followed by stronger structural support at the 20-day SMA around 6.21. A break below this zone could expose the psychological 6.00 level. On the upside, resistance is located at 6.3279, aligned with the recent high, with a breakout potentially opening room toward 6.40. Overall, price is compressing between tight boundaries, signaling an imminent expansion phase.

Trading strategy favors range-bound positioning until a decisive breakout occurs. Short-term traders may consider buying near 6.21–6.28 support with tight risk controls, targeting 6.33 and 6.40 on rebounds. Alternatively, a breakout above 6.33 with volume could confirm bullish continuation, while a breakdown below 6.21 would invalidate the near-term positive bias. Given low volatility, disciplined position sizing and stop placement are essential.
